---
title: "Working With The Document Viewer"
---

The Document Viewer is the area where you can view an enlarged version of each page of every document. You can navigate between the pages and customize the page view settings as per your requirements. You can also verify document metadata by hovering on the fields listed. Each field is highlighted on the page in the Document Viewer.

The Document Viewer toolbar enables you to adjust the display magnification of each document page, rotate a page, and also navigate between document pages using the following commands.

| Toolbar Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Displays thumbnail images of the total pages in a document. You can also press Shift + T. |
|  | Displays the previous page in the document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. You can also press Shift + P. |
| `<Current page>/<Total number of pages>` | Type the number of the page where you want to navigate. |
|  | Displays the next page in document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. You can also press Shift + N. |
|  | Displays the page as an image. You can also press Shift + I. |
|  | Displays only the extracted text from the document. You can also press Shift + W. |
|  | Zooms out. You can also press Shift + Alt + -. |
|  | Zooms in. You can also press Shift + Alt + +. |
|  | Rotates the image clockwise. You can also press Shift + R. |
|  | Fits the current image to the width or height of the Document Viewer area so that maximum enlargement is possible. You can also press Shift + F. |
|  | Displays the list of keyboard shortcuts. |
